children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: In consultation with the Director and in accordance with the guidance established under paragraph (2), the head of each agency shall, to the maximum extent practicable, develop and maintain a comprehensive data inventory that accounts for all data assets created by, collected by, under the control or direction of, or maintained by the agency. The head of each agency shall ensure that such inventory provides a clear and comprehensive understanding of the data assets in the possession of the agency.

children.label: i
children.text: A description of the data asset, including all variable names and definitions.

children.label: ii
children.text: The name or title of the data asset.

children.label: aa
children.text: an open Government data asset;

children.label: bb
children.text: subject to disclosure or partial disclosure or exempt from disclosure under ;section 552 of title 5

children.label: cc
children.text: a public data asset eligible for disclosure under subsection (b); or

children.label: dd
children.text: a data asset not subject to open format or open license requirements due to existing limitations or restrictions on government distribution of the asset; or

children.label: II
children.text: as of the date of such indication, has not made such determination.

children.label: iv
children.text: Any determination made under section 3582, if available.

children.label: v
children.text: A description of the method by which the public may access or request access to the data asset.

children.label: vi
children.text: The date on which the data asset was most recently updated.

children.label: vii
children.text: Each agency responsible for maintaining the data asset.

children.label: viii
children.text: The owner of the data asset.

children.label: ix
children.text: To the extent practicable, any restriction on the use of the data asset.

children.label: x
children.text: The location of the data asset.

children.label: xi
children.text: Any other metadata necessary to make the comprehensive data inventory useful to the agency and the public, or otherwise determined useful by the Director.

children.label: B
children.text: A requirement for the head of an agency to exclude from the comprehensive data inventory any data asset contained on a national security system, as defined in .section 11103 of title 40

children.label: C
children.text: Criteria for the head of an agency to use in determining which metadata required by subparagraph (A), if any, in the comprehensive data inventory may not be made publicly available, which shall include, at a minimum, a requirement to ensure all information that could not otherwise be withheld from disclosure under is made public in the comprehensive data inventory.section 552 of title 5

children.label: D
children.text: A requirement for the head of each agency, in accordance with a procedure established by the Director, to submit for inclusion in the Federal data catalogue maintained under subsection (c) the comprehensive data inventory developed pursuant to subparagraph (C), including any real-time updates to such inventory, and data assets made available in accordance with subparagraph (E) or any electronic hyperlink providing access to such data assets.

children.label: i
children.text: risks and restrictions related to the disclosure of personally identifiable information, including the risk that an individual data asset in isolation does not pose a privacy or confidentiality risk but when combined with other available information may pose such a risk;

children.label: ii
children.text: security considerations, including the risk that information in an individual data asset in isolation does not pose a security risk but when combined with other available information may pose such a risk;

children.label: iii
children.text: the cost and benefits to the public of converting the data into a format that could be understood and used by the public;

children.label: iv
children.text: whether the public dissemination of the data asset could result in legal liability;

children.label: I
children.text: is subject to intellectual property rights, including rights under titles 17 and 35;

children.label: II
children.text: contains confidential business information, that could be withheld under ; orsection 552(b)(4) of title 5

children.label: III
children.text: is restricted by contract or other binding, written agreement;

children.label: vi
children.text: whether the holder of a right to such data asset has been consulted;

children.label: vii
children.text: the expectation that all data assets that would otherwise be made available under be disclosed; andsection 552 of title 5

children.label: viii
children.text: any other considerations that the Director determines to be relevant.

children.label: F
children.text: Criteria for the head of an agency to use in assessing the indication of a determination under subparagraph (A)(iii) and how to prioritize any such subsequent determinations in the strategic information management plan under section 3506, in consideration of the existing resources available to the agency.

children.heading: .—Regular updates required
children.label: 3 .—Regular updates required
children.text: With respect to each data asset created or identified by an agency, the head of the agency shall update the comprehensive data inventory of the agency not later than 90 days after the date of such creation or identification.

children.heading: .—Public Data Assets
children.label: b .—Public Data Assets
children.text: The head of each agency shall submit public data assets, or links to public data assets available online, as open Government data assets for inclusion in the Federal data catalogue maintained under subsection (c), in accordance with the guidance established under subsection (a)(2).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator of General Services shall maintain a single public interface online as a point of entry dedicated to sharing agency data assets with the public, which shall be known as the “Federal data catalogue”. The Administrator and the Director shall ensure that agencies can submit public data assets, or links to public data assets, for publication and public availability on the interface.

children.label: A
children.text: include any definitions, regulations, policies, checklists, and case studies related to open data policy;

children.label: B
children.text: facilitate collaboration and the adoption of best practices across the Federal Government relating to the adoption of open data practices; and

children.label: C
children.text: be made available on the Federal data catalogue maintained under paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Access to other data assets
children.label: 3 .—Access to other data assets
children.text: The Director shall ensure the Federal data catalogue maintained under paragraph (1) provides information on how the public can access a data asset included in a comprehensive data inventory under subsection (a) that is not yet available on the Federal data catalogue, including information regarding the application process established under .section 3583 of title 44

children.heading: .—Delegation
children.label: d .—Delegation
children.text: The Director shall delegate to the Administrator of the Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs and the Administrator of the Office of Electronic Government the authority to jointly issue guidance required under this section.
